key_points: Climate is determined by long-term patterns of temperature, pressure, winds, humidity and precipitation
India's climate is influenced by several climatic controls including latitude, altitude, pressure systems, distance from the sea, ocean currents and relief features
The Himalayas play a crucial role by blocking cold winds from Central Asia and forcing monsoon winds to rise causing rainfall
Pressure differences between land and sea lead to seasonal reversal of winds known as monsoon winds
The hot weather season is characterized by high temperatures, dust storms and hot dry winds called loo
The southwest monsoon winds bring most of the rainfall to India beginning in early June
The monsoon splits into two branches: the Arabian Sea branch and the Bay of Bengal branch
Rainfall distribution in India varies greatly due to relief features and monsoon wind patterns
The retreating monsoon season is associated with cyclonic storms in the Bay of Bengal
Monsoon rainfall plays a vital role in agriculture and the Indian economy
examples: Example of heavy orographic rainfall: Khasi Hills in Meghalaya
Example of rain shadow region: Deccan Plateau behind Western Ghats
Example of cold desert: Ladakh in the rain shadow of the Himalayas
Example of winter rainfall source: Western disturbances from Mediterranean region
Example of monsoon arrival: Kerala coast in early June
Example of retreating monsoon rainfall: Tamil Nadu coast
formulas: Monsoon mechanism = Seasonal pressure differences + moisture-laden winds
Rain shadow = Area receiving little rainfall due to mountain barrier
Orographic rainfall = Moist air forced to rise over mountains leading to precipitation
Continentality = Extreme climate in inland areas due to lack of ocean influence
exam_tips: Clearly explain the climatic controls affecting India's climate in exams
Use examples such as Mawsynram, Ladakh and Rajasthan when explaining rainfall distribution
Understand the mechanism of southwest monsoon winds
Study the characteristics of each season in India carefully
Link climate topics with agriculture and economic activities for better answers
